summary: Phase-2 Regelchunk: travel aus TSR Inc - AD&D 2nd Edition - PHBR05 - The Complete Psionics Handbook.pdf.

source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 73 | score=10] dimensional door: 1) a dimension walk is not physically traumatizing and 2) the range of travel is greater. On the other hand, dimen- sion walk only allows the psionicist himself to travel, and he can easily get lost. The dimension walker opens up a vaguely shimmering portal. Only the psionidst who opened the portal can enter it. As soon as he does, the portal closes behind him. He finds himself engulfed by an inky grayness that is virtually without features. The effect is one of extreme sensory deprivation. The psionicist can travel through this gray realm at a speed of 7 leagues (21 miles) per turn. He cannot see where he is or where he’s going while in the realm. He has only his in- stinc
[PDF_PAGE 67 | score=8] I Psychoportat ion 1 Psychoportive Sciences Banishment Power Score: Int -1 Initial Cost: 30 Maintenance Cost: lO/round Range: 5 yards Preparation Time: 0 Area of Effect: individual Prerequisites: teleport With this power, the psionidst can teleport a creature against its will to a pocket dimen- sion and hold it there. The creature being ban- ished must be extremely close-within 5 yards. The pocket dimension is a featureless area with a benign environment-it may be hot or cold, dark or I&, but not so much that it will cause injury. Banishment has a boomerang feature. If the psionicist fails to pay the pay the maintenance cost, the banished creature automatically re- turns to its original loca
[PDF_PAGE 112 | score=4] ferent from magic. Psionic guilds and secret associations can be found in major cities. Krynn, a DRAGONLANCP game setting. No natives of Kqmn exhibit any psionic po- tential whatsoever. What few psionicists live on that world undoubtedly came from some- where else (via spelljamming vessels or other magical travel) or are the descendants of psionically-able ancestors who came from an- other world. Only the most widely-read sages and wizards will have any knowledge of psi- onics. RAVENLOFT”’ game setting. Psionics is known in Ravenloft. However, the nature of the Demiplane of Dread restricts the effective- ness of some psionic powers: No psionic power can breach the Mist. A character can telep
[PDF_PAGE 126 | score=4] t 1 Many creatures in the AD&P Monstrous Compendium series boast psionic powers. The text below brings the monsters up to date with the rules presented in this book. The creatures appear in alphabetical order. (Creatures from the Outer Planes are listed separately.) Each entry is organized as follows: Level-How tough the monster is, in terms of a psioni- cist's experience level. DUScUDev-How many disciplines the creature can ac- cess, followed by the total number of sciences and deve tions the creature knows (in all accessible disciplines). Attack/Defense-Telepathic attack and defense modes thecreaturecanuse. (Defensemodesarenot includedin the total number of powers the creature knows.) Abbr
[PDF_PAGE 75 | score=3] tination. Such dream adventures should mini- mize combat and maximize problems, puzzles, and surreal atmosphere. If combat dog occur, it has no effect on the character physically, but if he is killed, he wakes up, right where he started. All dream journeys take approximately eight hours to complete, successful or not. Passengers: The psionicist can bring other characters along with him in the dream. His power score is reduced by one for each passen- ger he carries. Passengers must also be sleep- ing. A single power check launches everyone on the dream journey. If the check fails, they still attempt the dream journey but fall short of their target. The psionicist must pay the PSI' cost of the
[PDF_PAGE 98 | score=3] way identical to the original form, except that it has no substance. The psioniast can see and hear what the clone sees and hears. The clone will do anything the psionicist wants it to (as if it wete actually him). Furthermore, the psioni- cist's psyche goes with the clone, thus enabling it to perform all of his psionic powers. This power has a serious drawback. As long as the clone exists, the psionicist himself is practically a turnip. He retains only three senses: smell, taste, and touch. He cannot move, see, or hear. AU his psionic powers are transferred to the clone. In effect, the immo- bile character is no longer a psionicist; not even psionic sense will reveal his true nature. He can
[PDF_PAGE 40 | score=2] power check for him. If the check succeeds, the character knows which way he came. If the roll fails, he isn't sure. (He can still maintain the power normally, however.) Radial navigation can be helpful in several ways that are not obvious. For example, tele- portation and other extraordinary means of travel become simpler. Let's say a character cannot see a particular location because he's blindfolded. He leaves that location, but uses radial navigation to get a fix on it. That means he can still teleport back there. Furthermore, if the character has a fix on a place, he can reach it through the astral plane in just seven hours, the minimum possible (assuming of course that he can travel th
[PDF_PAGE 56 | score=2] only one hour. After that, if the by the target, nce Cost: l/tum none les polymorphing, but it er application. The psionicist elf into anything with approx- ame mass as his body: a wolf, a a chair, a rock, a tree. While in this e retains his own hit points and if possible, but gains the armor class sionicist also gains all rm allows, but no mag- .
